edibility: Choice edible — caps only (stems too tough); reviving with water after drying intensifies flavor; favored for soups and sauces.
search: Where/when to find — late spring–autumn after rain, in literal rings on lawns/meadows/golf courses; ID clues (buff cap with central bump, distant gills, tough flexible stem); DEADLY look-alikes include Clitocybe rivulosa (toxic, white) and Inocybe spp. (muscarinic poisoning).

toxicity.symptoms: N/A — not toxic if correctly identified; warning: lookalikes cause muscarinic syndrome (sweating, salivation, lacrimation).
toxicity.antidote: N/A — not toxic if correctly identified; for muscarinic look-alike: atropine.
